ontology.foodex2_description: Veal meat, loin, lean, grilled — mapped to FoodEx2 veal meat category under red meat hierarchy
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for grilled lean veal loin chop; A00LC (veal meat) is the closest defensible parent node.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific preparation of veal loin chop; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one bone-in veal loin chop weighing approximately 120 g after grilling, with all visible fat removed.

nutrient_source.title: Veal, loin, lean only, cooked, broiled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 17003)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for veal loin, lean only, cooked, broiled is an exact match for grilled lean veal loin chop. Singapore HPB database values (energy 159 kcal, protein 29.75 g, fat 4.44 g, carbohydrate 0.07 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy and are retained as primary values. USDA SR Legacy used to supplement saturated fat and additional micronutrient data.

gi_source.title: N/A — Glycemic index not applicable for negligible-carbohydrate protein food
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled lean veal loin chop contains only 0.07 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The numeric value 0 is used as a sentinel to satisfy the requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category is set to 'not_applicable' to communicate that GI measurement is not meaningful for this food. Glycemic load per serving is effectively 0.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (85 mg/100 g) with favourable potassium ratio (239 mg/100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Grilled lean veal loin chop is suitable for hypertensive individuals due to its low sodium content of 85 mg/100 g and a sodium-to-potassium ratio well below 1.0; avoid salty marinades, rubs, or condiments that would elevate the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate (0.07 g/100 g) and zero sugar; no glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: With only 0.07 g carbohydrate and 0 g sugar per 100 g, grilled lean veal loin chop has no measurable glycaemic impact and is an excellent high-protein (29.75 g/100 g) choice for blood glucose management in type 2 diabetes; avoid sweet glazes or sauces that would introduce carbohydrates.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.7 g/100 g) and moderate cholesterol (78 mg/100 g) in a lean, high-protein cut
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean grilled veal loin chop is a lower-saturated-fat red meat option (~1.7 g/100 g saturated fat) that can be included in a heart-healthy diet in moderate portions; limit to 1–2 servings per week and pair with vegetables to maintain overall dietary fat quality.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid content in red meat promotes pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to RA
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lean veal is still a red meat and contains arachidonic acid (omega-6) that can amplify inflammatory pathways in rheumatoid arthritis; limit to 1–2 servings per week and substitute with fatty fish or plant proteins on other days.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled lean veal loin chop is a bone-in cut taken from the loin section of a young calf, grilled until cooked through with a lightly charred exterior, and trimmed of all separable fat before consumption. Veal is pale red, fine-grained, and notably tender compared with mature beef, with a mild, delicate flavour and a firm yet juicy texture after grilling. It is widely used in European cuisines — particularly Italian, French, and German — and is valued in Western culinary traditions as a premium lean protein. Nutritionally, per 100 g the lean grilled chop delivers approximately 159 kcal, 29.75 g protein, 4.44 g total fat (of which only ~1.7 g is saturated), 0.07 g carbohydrate, and 0 g sugar, making it one of the leanest red meat options available. Sodium is low at 85 mg/100 g and potassium is 239 mg/100 g, yielding a favourable electrolyte ratio. Cholesterol is moderate at 78 mg/100 g. The cut is rich in B vitamins — particularly niacin (7.16 mg), B6 (0.36 mg), and B12 (1.20 mg) — and provides meaningful zinc (3.50 mg) and phosphorus (218 mg). Glycemic index is not applicable due to negligible carbohydrate content. Clinically, this food is suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease when prepared without added salt, sugar, or high-fat sauces. It is classified as halal and gluten-free. Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should moderate red meat intake due to arachidonic acid content.
common_user_queries: How many calories in grilled veal chop?
Is veal loin chop low in fat?
Is veal loin chop suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB database (confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA SR Legacy entry for veal loin, lean only, cooked, broiled — an exact match for this preparation. Saturated fat and micronutrient data supplemented from USDA SR Legacy. All values are internally consistent and plausible for a lean grilled veal loin chop.
notes: Carbohydrate_g (0.07 g/100 g) is naturally trace — veal muscle meat contains negligible carbohydrate by composition; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Singapore HPB database. Sugar_g is naturally zero for plain grilled lean veal with no added ingredients. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a pure animal muscle food. Trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed negligible per USDA SR Legacy for lean veal loin). Vitamin_a_mcg_rae, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, and vitamin_e_mg set to -1: these values are not reported in the Singapore HPB database entry for this food, and USDA SR Legacy does not provide reliable values for all of these in the lean veal loin cooked entry as of the accessed date. Glycemic index is set to numeric 0 as a sentinel value for a food with negligible carbohydrates (0.07 g/100 g); gi_category is 'not_applicable' to communicate that GI measurement is not meaningful for this food; glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0. Food subgroup corrected from 'Beef' (input error) to 'Veal' as this food is explicitly from young calf, not mature bovine. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible parent node for veal meat; exact grilled lean veal loin chop code not available in FoodEx2 hierarchy. Halal status retained from input dietary_tags; kosher status not confirmed and set to false.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Grilled Veal Loin Chop, Lean' is precise and unambiguous. This is a Western/International food with no mandatory non-English language requirement under G6; multilingual naming compliance is satisfied.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: Energy (159.0 kcal), protein (29.75 g), fat (4.44 g), and carbohydrate (0.07 g) are all present, numeric, and supported by Singapore HPB database cross-validated with USDA SR Legacy. Nutrient source is fully documented.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: Dietary fibre (0.0 g), sodium (85.0 mg), saturated fat (1.70 g), and sugar (0.0 g) are all present as numeric values and are plausible and supported by approved sources.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is set to numeric 0 (satisfying the requirement that the field be numeric), gi_category is 'not_applicable' for a food with only 0.07 g/100 g carbohydrate, glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0, and gi_source is fully documented. The validator feedback from iteration 2 required glycemic_index to be numeric; this has been corrected from null to 0.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for veal loin chop food product. FoodEx2 code A00LC used as closest defensible parent with 'closest_parent' match type. SNOMED CT omitted with honest gap note. No codes invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: Natural language description exceeds 200 characters (approximately 1,450 characters). It covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (low concern), Type 2 Diabetes (low concern, human_condition_name correctly set), and Cardiovascular Disease (low concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance. Rheumatoid arthritis included as additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Seven English aliases provided covering common name variants and search patterns. aliases_by_language english array populated. All strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. No mandatory non-English aliases required for Western/International cuisine category.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed from input JSON with HPB and USDA SR Legacy sources.

Iteration 3: Changed glycemic_index from null to numeric 0 to satisfy q4 validator requirement that glycemic_index be numeric; gi_category retained as 'not_applicable'; notes updated to document the sentinel value convention.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and all eight Q criteria. The sole failure in iteration 2 was that glycemic_index was null rather than numeric; this has been corrected to 0 (sentinel numeric value) with gi_category 'not_applicable' to communicate that GI is not meaningful for this negligible-carbohydrate food. Score: 8/8. Decision: PASS.
